summary: This lecture discusses the topic of voluntary fasting (Sawm al-Tatawwu') from the book "Al-Wajeez fi Fiqh al-Sunnah" (The Concise in the Jurisprudence of the Sunnah). The speaker explains the Prophet Muhammad's fasting practices, particularly his extensive fasting during the month of Sha'ban, citing the hadith of Aisha recorded in Sahih Bukhari and Sahih Muslim. The lecture emphasizes that the believer should take advantage of periods of spiritual enthusiasm for good deeds while maintaining a minimum standard during less motivated periods. The speaker then addresses the specific practice of fasting on Mondays and Thursdays, citing a hadith from Sunan Abu Dawud, explaining that the Prophet fasted regularly on these two days because the deeds of servants are presented to Allah on these days.
suggested_title: Voluntary Fasting and the Practice of Fasting on Mondays and Thursdays - Al-Wajeez Fi Fiqh al-Sunnah
hadith_chapter: Book on Fasting (Sawm) - Voluntary Fasting and the Practice of Fasting on Mondays and Thursdays
catchy_title: Why Did the Prophet Fast Mondays and Thursdays?
